citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
Around each of the 3 buildings used for housing dogs, there are numerous tall (at least 20” tall), bushy weeds. There were
2 tall weeds at the edge of the cement pad of the for the outdoor part of the shelter building housing females with litters of
puppies.
The building next to it had several of the tall bushy weeds next to the drain. The path to the cement building housing small
dogs had very tall weeds (at least 6’ tall) on each side of the path and up to the building. There were numerous thick tall
weeds on the opposite side of the building from the entrance that touched the wall and were above the level of the bottom
of the window.
The heavy growth of weeds around the buildings makes it difficult to keep the building and grounds clean and in good
repair. It also makes it difficult to reduce or eliminate breeding & living areas of rodents and other pests and vermin.
The licensee must remove weeds, grasses and bushes and control their growth so as to facilitate cleaning of the
premises and pest control, and to protect the health and well-being of the animals.
Correction date: 20 May 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.11(c) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
Around each of the 3 buildings used for housing dogs, there are numerous tall (at least 20” tall), bushy weeds. There were 
2 tall weeds at the edge of the cement pad of the for the outdoor part of the shelter building housing females with litters of 
puppies.  
The building next to it had several of the tall bushy weeds next to the drain. The path to the cement building housing small 
dogs had very tall weeds (at least 6’ tall) on each side of the path and up to the building. There were numerous thick tall 
weeds on the opposite side of the building from the entrance that touched the wall and were above the level of the bottom 
of the window. 
The heavy growth of weeds around the buildings makes it difficult to keep the building and grounds clean and in good 
repair. It also makes it difficult to reduce or eliminate breeding & living areas of rodents and other pests and vermin.  
The licensee must remove weeds, grasses and bushes and control their growth so as to facilitate cleaning of the 
premises and pest control, and to protect the health and well-being of the animals.  
Correction date: 20 May 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
